title: Replace, Case & Join
intro: Quick edits without entering Insert mode: `r` replaces a single character, `~` toggles the case of the character under the cursor, `J` joins the next line onto the current one. `cc` changes an entire line.
summary: Quick edits without leaving Normal mode.

exercises.instruction: There's a typo: `hxllo` should be `hello`. The cursor is on the `x` — replace it with `re`.
hints: `r` replaces the single character under the cursor with the next key you press.

exercises.instruction: Capitalize the first letter: the cursor is on `h` — toggle its case with `~`.
hints: `~` flips the case of the character under the cursor and moves right.

exercises.instruction: Join these two lines into one with `J` (a space is added automatically).
hints: `J` (Shift+j) joins the line below onto the current line.

exercises.instruction: Rewrite the whole line: press `cc`, type `const y = 2;`, then `Esc`.
hints: `cc` clears the entire line and drops you into Insert mode at the right indent.

exercises.instruction: Fix both typos: reach each `x` with `fx` and replace it — `re` for the first, `ro` for the second.
Join all three lines into one: press `J` twice.
hints: Each `J` pulls the next line up with a single joining space.

exercises.instruction: Capitalize the whole word: `~` toggles case and moves right — press it five times.
hints: `~` advances automatically, so you never need to move the cursor.
